behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: The failure is primarily whether a real speech-to-text model correctly performs diarization/speaker labeling on real meeting audio. A stubbed transcript would hide diarization failures. Rejected Workflow: behavior text is about preserving labels in STT output; it does not require validating multiple model decisions in sequence if the assertion is made at the STT output boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Run real meeting audio through the real STT API and assert transcript segments include stable speaker labels mapped to utterances; then verify the summarizer can consume those labels for attendee attribution. Use real model output, not canned transcript fixtures.

behaviors.description: The structured summary contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: This is an LLM output-quality requirement (recall of assignees/tasks). Deterministic mocks can validate schema/routing but cannot catch missed action items from real model judgment. Rejected Workflow: this behavior can be validated with a fixed transcript plus one real summarization model step, so multiple agentic stages are not required.
behaviors.test_description: Provide transcripts with known attendee-task assignments to the real LLM summarizer and assert each assigned attendee appears with at least one action item in structured output across repeated runs/tolerance thresholds.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the "decisions" section of the summary, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM extraction and section placement behavior. A mock response would always satisfy section placement and miss real omissions/misclassification. Rejected Workflow: one real summarization decision on a prepared transcript is sufficient to expose this failure mode.
behaviors.test_description: Use transcripts containing explicit decision statements and evaluate real LLM summaries for presence of those decisions specifically under the structured `decisions` field/section, not only in free-text narrative.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is about sequential multi-stage processing with handoff across segment-level model outputs and final merged summary coherence. It involves multiple agentic decisions in sequence, so Agentic-only is too low. Rejected System-only: real subprocess/OS concerns may exist, but behavior text emphasizes sequential agent pipeline correctness and coherent merged output across stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real >60-minute recording through production chunking settings, execute per-segment transcription/summarization in sequence, then perform merge/final synthesis; assert no segment loss/duplication and that merged output remains coherent and structurally valid end-to-end.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline — upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output — completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly an end-to-end pipeline with multiple real AI stages (transcription + summarization) plus delivery. Real failures emerge from cross-stage orchestration and model-dependent outputs over a full run. Rejected System: system-level infra checks alone do not validate multi-agentic pipeline behavior and handoffs required by the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a production-like end-to-end run on a real meeting recording: ingest/upload, real STT call, real LLM summarization, and output delivery. Assert completion status, valid structured schema, and no manual retries/interventions.
